usage_notes: Normally uncountable; plural form exists but is rarely used
common_collocations: es regnet, im Regen stehen, starker Regen
image_context: Rain falling on a street with puddles
example_1: Es gibt heute viel Regen.
example_1_translation: Hoje tem muita chuva.
example_2: Ich mag keinen starken Regen.
example_2_translation: Eu não gosto de chuva forte.
example_3: Nach dem Regen scheint die Sonne.
example_3_translation: Depois da chuva, o sol brilha.
pronunciation_tips: Long 'e' in 'Regen': /eː/; final '-en' is a reduced /ən/
memory_hook: Regen sounds like 'rain' with a German accent
related_words: regnen, der Regenschirm, die Wolke, nass
common_mistakes: Using plural 'Regen' — it is almost always used as uncountable

usage_notes: Plural is very rare; usually used in the singular
common_collocations: die Sonne scheint, in der Sonne liegen
image_context: A bright sun in a clear blue sky
example_1: Die Sonne scheint heute sehr hell.
example_1_translation: O sol está brilhando muito forte hoje.
example_2: Wir liegen in der Sonne am Strand.
example_2_translation: Nós ficamos deitados ao sol na praia.
example_3: Ohne Sonne ist der Winter sehr dunkel.
example_3_translation: Sem sol, o inverno é muito escuro.
pronunciation_tips: Initial 's' before a vowel is voiced /z/ in German
memory_hook: Sonne sounds like 'sunny' — the thing that makes it sunny
related_words: sonnig, der Sonnenschein, das Licht, warm
common_mistakes: Pronouncing initial 's' as /s/ instead of /z/

common_collocations: starker Wind, der Wind weht, im Wind stehen
image_context: Trees bending in strong wind
example_1: Heute weht ein starker Wind.
example_1_translation: Hoje faz muito vento.
example_2: Der Wind kommt aus dem Norden.
example_2_translation: O vento vem do norte.
example_3: Bei so viel Wind ist es kalt.
example_3_translation: Com tanto vento está frio.
pronunciation_tips: 'W' in German is /v/, not /w/; final 'd' is devoiced to /t/
memory_hook: Wind is the same word in English and German — identical spelling
related_words: windig, der Sturm, wehen, kalt
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'W' as /w/ instead of /v/

common_collocations: dunkle Wolken, die Wolken ziehen auf, bewölkt
image_context: White and grey clouds in the sky
example_1: Am Himmel sind viele dunkle Wolken.
example_1_translation: No céu há muitas nuvens escuras.
example_2: Die Wolken kommen von Westen.
example_2_translation: As nuvens vêm do oeste.
example_3: Heute ist es bewölkt und kühl.
example_3_translation: Hoje está nublado e frio.
pronunciation_tips: 'W' is /v/; 'o' is short /ɔ/; final '-e' is a weak /ə/
memory_hook: Wolke — think of 'voluminous' clouds, or the German 'Himmelswolke' (sky cloud)
related_words: bewölkt, der Himmel, regnen, der Regen
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'W' as /w/ instead of /v/

common_collocations: die Temperatur messen, bei hohen Temperaturen
image_context: A thermometer showing outdoor temperature
example_1: Die Temperatur liegt heute bei zwanzig Grad.
example_1_translation: A temperatura está em vinte graus hoje.
example_2: Im Winter sinkt die Temperatur stark.
example_2_translation: No inverno a temperatura cai muito.
example_3: Bei welcher Temperatur gefällt dir das Wetter?
example_3_translation: Em qual temperatura você gosta do tempo?
pronunciation_tips: Stress on the last syllable: tem-pe-ra-TUR
memory_hook: Almost identical to English 'temperature' — easy cognate
related_words: der Grad, heiß, kalt, warm, das Thermometer
common_mistakes: Stressing an early syllable instead of the final '-tur'

usage_notes: Uncountable; 'Schneefälle' is used when talking about snowfalls (plural events)
common_collocations: es schneit, Schnee fällt, im Schnee spielen
image_context: Snow falling on a winter landscape
example_1: Im Winter gibt es viel Schnee in den Bergen.
example_1_translation: No inverno há muita neve nas montanhas.
example_2: Die Kinder spielen im Schnee.
example_2_translation: As crianças brincam na neve.
example_3: Heute Nacht soll Schnee fallen.
example_3_translation: Esta noite deve cair neve.
pronunciation_tips: 'Sch' is always /ʃ/; 'ee' is a long /eː/
memory_hook: Schnee sounds like 'sh-nay' — imagine saying 'snowy' with a German accent
related_words: schneien, der Winter, kalt, der Schneemann
common_mistakes: Adding a plural: 'Schnees' does not work — it is uncountable

common_collocations: ein Gewitter zieht auf, es gibt ein Gewitter
image_context: Dark storm clouds with lightning over a landscape
example_1: Am Abend zieht ein Gewitter auf.
example_1_translation: À noite, uma tempestade se aproxima.
example_2: Das Gewitter war sehr laut.
example_2_translation: A tempestade foi muito barulhenta.
example_3: Bei Gewitter bleibe ich lieber drinnen.
example_3_translation: Com tempestade, prefiro ficar dentro de casa.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: ge-WIT-ter; 'tt' doubles the short vowel /ɪ/
memory_hook: Gewitter — 'Witter' reminds of 'weather' — stormy weather
related_words: der Blitz, der Donner, der Sturm, der Regen
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'Sturm' — a Gewitter specifically involves thunder and lightning

usage_notes: Follows standard adjective declension; comparative: wärmer; superlative: am wärmsten
common_collocations: warmes Wetter, es ist warm, sich warm anziehen
image_search_query: warm sunny weather people outdoors Germany
image_context: People enjoying warm weather outdoors
example_1: Das Wetter ist heute sehr warm.
example_1_translation: O tempo está muito quente hoje.
example_2: Im Sommer sind die Tage schön warm.
example_2_translation: No verão os dias são agradavelmente quentes.
example_3: Zieh dich warm an, es ist kalt draußen.
example_3_translation: Vista-se bem, está frio lá fora.
pronunciation_tips: 'W' is /v/; 'a' is short /a/; final 'm' is /m/
memory_hook: Very similar to English 'warm' — easy cognate
related_words: heiß, kalt, die Wärme, der Sommer
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'W' as /w/ instead of /v/

common_collocations: es ist kalt, kaltes Wetter, kalt werden
image_context: A frosty winter scene with snow and ice
example_1: Im Januar ist es hier sehr kalt.
example_1_translation: Em janeiro está muito frio aqui.
example_2: Ich friere, weil es kalt ist.
example_2_translation: Estou com frio porque está frio.
example_3: Das kalte Wasser erfrischt mich.
example_3_translation: A água fria me refresca.
pronunciation_tips: Short 'a' /a/; final 't' is unaspirated and slightly softened
memory_hook: Kalt sounds like 'cold' — very close English cognate
related_words: die Kälte, warm, frieren, der Winter
common_mistakes: Forgetting umlaut in comparative: 'kälter', not 'kalter'

usage_notes: Plural exists but is rarely used; commonly uncountable
common_collocations: dichter Nebel, Nebel liegt über der Stadt
image_context: A misty foggy morning landscape
example_1: Heute Morgen liegt dichter Nebel über der Stadt.
example_1_translation: Esta manhã há um nevoeiro denso sobre a cidade.
example_2: Bei Nebel muss man langsam fahren.
example_2_translation: Com nevoeiro, é preciso dirigir devagar.
example_3: Der Nebel löst sich am Mittag auf.
example_3_translation: O nevoeiro se dissipa ao meio-dia.
pronunciation_tips: Long 'e' in 'Ne-': /eː/; final '-el' is /əl/
memory_hook: Nebel — sounds like 'nebulous' meaning foggy and unclear
related_words: neblig, der Dunst, die Sicht, grau
common_mistakes: Using 'Nebel' with a plural form — it is typically uncountable

common_collocations: ein Sturm zieht auf, im Sturm, schwerer Sturm
image_search_query: storm strong wind dark clouds rain
image_context: Dark storm clouds with heavy rain and wind
example_1: Ein starker Sturm trifft die Küste.
example_1_translation: Uma forte tempestade atinge a costa.
example_2: Der Sturm hat viele Bäume umgeworfen.
example_2_translation: A tempestade derrubou muitas árvores.
example_3: Wir bleiben wegen des Sturms zu Hause.
example_3_translation: Ficamos em casa por causa da tempestade.
pronunciation_tips: 'St' at the start is /ʃt/; 'u' is short /ʊ/
memory_hook: Sturm sounds like 'storm' — very close English cognate
related_words: das Gewitter, der Wind, der Regen, stürmisch
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'St' as /st/ instead of /ʃt/

usage_notes: Context distinguishes: 'Eis auf der Straße' = ice on the road; 'ein Eis' = one ice cream
common_collocations: Eis auf der Straße, Schlittschuhe auf dem Eis
image_search_query: ice frost frozen winter Germany road
image_context: Icy road surface with frost in winter
example_1: Bei Eis auf der Straße fährt man vorsichtig.
example_1_translation: Com gelo na rua, dirige-se com cuidado.
example_2: Der See ist im Winter mit Eis bedeckt.
example_2_translation: O lago está coberto de gelo no inverno.
example_3: Das Eis auf dem Bürgersteig ist gefährlich.
example_3_translation: O gelo na calçada é perigoso.
pronunciation_tips: 'ei' is /aɪ/ as in 'eye'; final 's' is /s/
memory_hook: Eis is close to 'ice' — same meaning, very similar sound
related_words: frieren, kalt, der Winter, der Frost
common_mistakes: Confusing Eis (ice/weather) with Eis (ice cream) — context is key

common_collocations: harter Frost, Frost in der Nacht, bei Frost
image_search_query: frost ice crystals plants morning Germany
image_context: Ice crystals of frost covering plants and grass
example_1: In der Nacht gibt es Frost.
example_1_translation: À noite há geada.
example_2: Der Frost hat die Blumen beschädigt.
example_2_translation: A geada danificou as flores.
example_3: Bei starkem Frost muss man die Pflanzen schützen.
example_3_translation: Com geada forte, é preciso proteger as plantas.
pronunciation_tips: 'Fr' is /fʁ/; 'o' is short /ɔ/; final 'st' is /st/
memory_hook: Frost is spelled and pronounced almost identically to English 'frost'
related_words: frieren, das Eis, kalt, der Winter
common_mistakes: Confusing Frost (frost/freeze) with Eis (ice on surfaces)

usage_notes: Both meanings (sky and heaven) use the same word
common_collocations: am Himmel, blauer Himmel, der Himmel ist bewölkt
image_context: A wide blue sky with scattered clouds
example_1: Der Himmel ist heute strahlend blau.
example_1_translation: O céu está radiante e azul hoje.
example_2: Am Himmel kann man Sterne sehen.
example_2_translation: No céu dá para ver estrelas.
example_3: Der Himmel war voller dunkler Wolken.
example_3_translation: O céu estava cheio de nuvens escuras.
pronunciation_tips: 'H' is aspirated /h/; 'i' is short /ɪ/; '-el' is /əl/
memory_hook: Himmel — think 'heaven' and 'sky' are both above us
related_words: die Wolke, die Sonne, der Stern, blau
common_mistakes: Forgetting plural is the same as singular

usage_notes: Almost always uncountable; 'Witterung' is a more formal synonym
common_collocations: das Wetter ist schön, schlechtes Wetter, Wettervorhersage
image_search_query: weather forecast symbols sunny cloudy rain Germany
image_context: Weather forecast symbols showing various conditions
example_1: Wie ist das Wetter heute?
example_1_translation: Como está o tempo hoje?
example_2: Das Wetter in Hamburg ist oft regnerisch.
example_2_translation: O tempo em Hamburgo é frequentemente chuvoso.
example_3: Bei gutem Wetter machen wir einen Ausflug.
example_3_translation: Com bom tempo, fazemos um passeio.
pronunciation_tips: 'W' is /v/; double 't' shortens the preceding vowel /ɛ/
memory_hook: Wetter sounds like 'weather' with a German 'W' = /v/ sound
related_words: die Wettervorhersage, regnen, sonnig, kalt
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'W' as /w/ instead of /v/

usage_notes: Plural is identical to singular when used with a number: 'zehn Grad'
common_collocations: zwanzig Grad Celsius, minus fünf Grad
image_context: A thermometer showing temperature in degrees Celsius
example_1: Es sind heute dreißig Grad.
example_1_translation: Hoje estão trinta graus.
example_2: Minus zehn Grad — das ist sehr kalt!
example_2_translation: Menos dez graus — isso é muito frio!
example_3: Die Temperatur steigt auf fünfundzwanzig Grad.
example_3_translation: A temperatura sobe para vinte e cinco graus.
pronunciation_tips: Final 'd' is devoiced to /t/; long 'a' in 'Grad': /aː/
memory_hook: Grad = grade/degree — same root as English 'grade'
related_words: die Temperatur, Celsius, das Thermometer, warm
common_mistakes: Adding a plural '-e': 'zehn Grad', not 'zehn Grade' (in temperature contexts)

common_collocations: es regnet, es hat geregnet, es wird regnen
image_context: Rain drops falling from a grey sky
example_1: Es regnet den ganzen Tag.
example_1_translation: Está chovendo o dia inteiro.
example_2: Gestern hat es stark geregnet.
example_2_translation: Ontem choveu muito forte.
example_3: Es soll morgen auch regnen.
example_3_translation: Amanhã também deve chover.
pronunciation_tips: Long 'e' in 'reg-': /eː/; 'gn' is /ɡn/ not /ŋ/
memory_hook: Regnen comes from 'Regen' (rain) — to do rain
related_words: der Regen, der Regenschirm, nass, die Wolke
common_mistakes: Using 'ich regne' — this verb is always impersonal: 'es regnet'

common_collocations: es schneit, es hat geschneit, stark schneien
image_context: Snowflakes falling in a winter scene
example_1: Es schneit seit heute Morgen.
example_1_translation: Está nevando desde esta manhã.
example_2: In den Alpen schneit es viel.
example_2_translation: Nos Alpes neva muito.
example_3: Gestern Nacht hat es geschneit.
example_3_translation: Ontem à noite nevou.
pronunciation_tips: 'Sch' is /ʃ/; 'ei' is /aɪ/
memory_hook: Schneien comes from Schnee (snow) — to do snow
related_words: der Schnee, der Winter, kalt, die Schneeflocke
common_mistakes: Using 'ich schneie' — always impersonal: 'es schneit'

common_collocations: sonniges Wetter, ein sonniger Tag
image_context: A bright sunny day with clear blue sky
example_1: Heute ist ein sonniger Tag.
example_1_translation: Hoje é um dia ensolarado.
example_2: Das Wetter ist sonnig und warm.
example_2_translation: O tempo está ensolarado e quente.
example_3: Im Sommer ist es hier meist sonnig.
example_3_translation: No verão, geralmente está ensolarado aqui.
pronunciation_tips: Initial 's' is voiced /z/; '-ig' at end of word is pronounced /ɪç/
memory_hook: Sonnig comes from Sonne (sun) — full of sun
related_words: die Sonne, scheinen, warm, der Sommer
common_mistakes: Pronouncing '-ig' as /-ɪɡ/ instead of /-ɪç/

common_collocations: bewölkter Himmel, es ist bewölkt, leicht bewölkt
image_context: An overcast grey sky with thick cloud cover
example_1: Der Himmel ist heute stark bewölkt.
example_1_translation: O céu está muito nublado hoje.
example_2: Es ist leicht bewölkt, aber warm.
example_2_translation: Está levemente nublado, mas quente.
example_3: Morgen wird es bewölkt und regnerisch.
example_3_translation: Amanhã estará nublado e chuvoso.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: be-WÖLKT; 'ö' is /œ/
memory_hook: Be-wölkt — 'Wolke' (cloud) is inside the word — covered with clouds
related_words: die Wolke, wolkig, der Himmel, grau
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'wolkig' — both mean cloudy, 'bewölkt' implies more coverage

common_collocations: einen Regenschirm mitnehmen, den Regenschirm aufspannen
image_context: A person holding an open umbrella in the rain
example_1: Vergiss deinen Regenschirm nicht.
example_1_translation: Não esqueça seu guarda-chuva.
example_2: Ich habe meinen Regenschirm vergessen.
example_2_translation: Eu esqueci meu guarda-chuva.
example_3: Mit dem Regenschirm bleibe ich trocken.
example_3_translation: Com o guarda-chuva fico seco.
pronunciation_tips: 'Regen' starts with /ʁ/; 'Schirm' has /ʃ/
memory_hook: Regen (rain) + Schirm (screen/shield) = rain shield
related_words: der Regen, regnen, nass, trocken
common_mistakes: Forgetting to take it — and forgetting the article is 'der'

usage_notes: The four seasons: Frühling, Sommer, Herbst, Winter
common_collocations: meine Lieblingsjahrzeit, die vier Jahreszeiten
image_search_query: four seasons spring summer autumn winter Germany
image_context: A collage showing the four seasons of the year
example_1: Meine Lieblingsjahreszeit ist der Sommer.
example_1_translation: Minha estação favorita é o verão.
example_2: Es gibt vier Jahreszeiten im Jahr.
example_2_translation: Há quatro estações no ano.
example_3: Im Herbst fallen die Blätter von den Bäumen.
example_3_translation: No outono as folhas caem das árvores.
pronunciation_tips: 'J' in German is /j/ as in English 'yes'; 'z' is /ts/
memory_hook: Jahr (year) + Zeit (time) = year-time = season
related_words: das Jahr, die Zeit, der Frühling, der Sommer
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'J' as /dʒ/ (English j) instead of /j/

common_collocations: heißes Wetter, es ist heiß, heiße Sonne
image_search_query: hot summer heat sun thermometer Germany
image_context: A hot summer day with the sun blazing down
example_1: Im Juli ist es sehr heiß.
example_1_translation: Em julho está muito quente.
example_2: Dieses Wasser ist zu heiß zum Trinken.
example_2_translation: Esta água está quente demais para beber.
example_3: Der heißeste Tag des Jahres war im August.
example_3_translation: O dia mais quente do ano foi em agosto.
pronunciation_tips: 'ei' is /aɪ/; 'ß' (Eszett) is a long /s/ sound
memory_hook: 'Heiß' and 'hot' — both start with 'h' and convey extreme heat
related_words: die Hitze, warm, schwül, der Sommer
common_mistakes: Confusing 'heiß' (very hot) with 'warm' (warm/pleasantly warm)

common_collocations: der Blitz schlägt ein, Blitz und Donner
image_context: A lightning bolt striking during a night storm
example_1: Der Blitz schlug in einen Baum ein.
example_1_translation: O raio atingiu uma árvore.
example_2: Bei Gewitter gibt es Blitz und Donner.
example_2_translation: Durante a tempestade há relâmpago e trovão.
example_3: Der Blitz leuchtete hell am Himmel.
example_3_translation: O relâmpago brilhou intensamente no céu.
pronunciation_tips: 'Bl' is /bl/; 'i' is short /ɪ/; 'tz' is /ts/
memory_hook: Blitz = 'blitz' in English (a lightning fast attack) — comes from the German word
related_words: der Donner, das Gewitter, der Sturm, blitzen
common_mistakes: Confusing Blitz (lightning) with Donner (thunder)

usage_notes: Typically uncountable; plural used for distinct claps of thunder
common_collocations: Blitz und Donner, lauter Donner, es donnert
image_search_query: thunder storm dark clouds rain lightning Germany
image_context: A dark stormy sky with lightning and the implication of thunder
example_1: Der Donner war so laut, dass ich erschrak.
example_1_translation: O trovão foi tão alto que me assustei.
example_2: Nach dem Blitz kommt der Donner.
example_2_translation: Depois do raio vem o trovão.
example_3: Bei Donner und Blitz bleibe ich im Haus.
example_3_translation: Com trovão e relâmpago fico em casa.
pronunciation_tips: Double 'n' shortens the preceding 'o' to /ɔ/; final '-er' is /ɐ/
memory_hook: Donner = Thor's thunder (Donnerstag = Thursday, Thor's day)
related_words: der Blitz, das Gewitter, donnern, der Sturm
common_mistakes: Confusing Donner (thunder) with Blitz (lightning) — thunder is the sound, lightning is the flash
